text:
Once upon a sunny afternoon in Willow Creek, a spunky parrot named Polly fluttered around the antique shelves looking for something interesting to say.

"Oh dear," Polly complained to no one in particular as she pecked at a dusty old book. "Isn't there something interesting in here?"

She spread her fiery red feathers wildly.

"Could it be?" she pondered. "Oh, look! I found this! 'The Adventures of the Scarlet Slug.'"

"Intriguing! And what would you think about a slug who could talk?"

"A slug?"

"Not particularly remarkable—it speaks."

"Prettiness all day!"

She flipped through the book. "Oh! I love snails. They're so charming.

They have these melodious calls—it's lovely listening to them."

Polly chirped her delight. "Maybe I could have a conversation with one?"

"Stay quiet—I hear it!"

Minutes later, a tiny, purple slug piped up.

"Hello?"

"Polly?"
